per_question.answer_preview: .context('message') adds static context string to error - simple and efficient. .with_context(|| format!('...')) lazily computes context only when error occurs - use for expensive context generation.

per_question.expected_preview: The bail! macro in Zed provides early error returns. Found in lmstudio.rs, vim/command.rs, agent_ui
per_question.answer_preview: bail!('message') is equivalent to return Err(anyhow!('message')) - it's a macro that returns early from the function with an error. Control flow: immediately exits function, no code after bail! execut

per_question.answer_preview: ZED_CLIENT_CHECKSUM_SEED is used to compute a checksum over telemetry events. The server verifies this checksum to validate events came from a legitimate Zed client (not spoofed). Computed in telemetr

per_question.answer_preview: use_key_equivalents allows keybindings to work based on physical key position rather than character produced. For non-QWERTY keyboards (Dvorak, AZERTY), pressing the physical Q position triggers bindi

per_question.answer_preview: util::defer(closure) returns a Deferred that runs closure when dropped - similar to Go's defer. Related to RAII: cleanup code runs on scope exit regardless of how (return, error, panic). In peer.rs: d

per_question.expected_preview: The buffer will panic with an assertion failure. The code explicitly asserts that transaction_depth
per_question.answer_preview: Calling end_transaction() when transaction_depth is 0 will panic. The implementation uses debug_assert! or explicit check: transaction_depth must be > 0. This invariant ensures transactions are proper

per_question.expected_preview: All three operations require that transaction_depth equals 0. The code enforces this with `assert_eq
per_question.answer_preview: These operations require transaction_depth == 0. You cannot push a new transaction while inside a transaction (push_transaction), nor can you undo/redo mid-transaction. This ensures atomic transaction

per_question.answer_preview: The has_conflict flag is set to true when file changes on disk AND buffer has unsaved edits. This flag prevents automatic reload - user must explicitly resolve. It signals: manual intervention needed,

per_question.expected_preview: The invariant is that start <= end always (selections are stored in canonical order). The set_head m
per_question.answer_preview: Selection invariant: start <= end in buffer coordinates (start is always before or equal to end). However, Selection tracks head (cursor) and tail (anchor) separately - head can be before or after tai

per_question.answer_preview: An edit is considered 'undone' based on undo_counts in UndoMap. Each edit operation has an undo count tracking how many times it's been undone. If undo_count for an operation is odd, the edit is curre

per_question.answer_preview: Operations with unresolvable anchors are deferred. The operation is queued (in deferred_ops or similar structure) until the required buffer version arrives. When missing operations arrive and are appl

per_question.expected_preview: Merged operations have their timestamps stored in the merged_operations vector (line 1377 in buffer.
per_question.answer_preview: When branch buffer merges into base, its operations are applied to base. Tracking merged operations is important because: 1) They become part of base's history, 2) Other branches need to know these op

per_question.expected_preview: always_deny takes absolute precedence over always_allow. According to the precedence documentation i
per_question.answer_preview: always_deny takes precedence over always_allow. If a command matches both patterns, it is denied. This fail-safe design prevents accidentally allowing dangerous commands. Order: 1) Check always_deny -

per_question.expected_preview: If any regex patterns in a tool's rules failed to compile (stored in invalid_patterns), the tool is
per_question.answer_preview: Invalid regex patterns in always_allow/always_deny are treated as non-matching (fail-safe). If a pattern can't compile, it's logged as warning but doesn't crash. Security feature because: malformed al

per_question.answer_preview: always_allow_tool_actions doesn't override: 1) Explicit always_deny patterns - denials always respected, 2) Operations outside tool system scope, 3) Extension capability checks (separate system), 4) C

per_question.answer_preview: Only the outermost transaction tracks was_dirty_before_starting_transaction. Nested transactions don't independently track dirty state - they're part of the outer transaction. Constraint: was_dirty_be

per_question.expected_preview: The disjoint field contains non-overlapping selections (line 31-32 in selections_collection.rs), whi
per_question.answer_preview: SelectionsCollection maintains disjoint (non-overlapping) selections as the canonical set. pending selection is a temporary selection being created (e.g., during drag). Guarantee: disjoint selections

per_question.answer_preview: check_invariants ensures fragment IDs are unique and properly ordered in the fragment tree. Fragment IDs are Lamport timestamps identifying when/who created each fragment. Critical because: 1) CRDT co

per_question.expected_preview: The diff is adjusted to account for intervening edits, and conflicting parts are discarded (line 227

per_question.expected_preview: When capability is Capability::ReadOnly (defined at line 87 in buffer.rs), the buffer cannot be edit
per_question.answer_preview: When capability is ReadOnly, Buffer rejects edit operations. Constraint enforced in edit methods: if !self.capability.can_edit() { return } or early error. Affects: insert/delete operations fail or no

per_question.expected_preview: The operation is ignored/rejected. Lines 3007-3011 in buffer.rs check if the stored selection set ha
per_question.answer_preview: UpdateSelections with older lamport_timestamp than stored selections is ignored. Each replica's selections have an associated timestamp. Only newer updates (higher timestamp) replace existing. This ha

per_question.expected_preview: push_empty_transaction creates a parent transaction without clearing the redo stack (lines 2448-2460
per_question.answer_preview: push_empty_transaction creates a transaction boundary without any edits. Purpose: mark a point in history for undo navigation even without text changes (e.g., after cursor movements, state changes). C

per_question.expected_preview: The group_interval field (default 300ms for production, 0 for tests per lines 196-200 in text.rs) de
per_question.answer_preview: History tracks transaction timestamps. When new transaction created, it checks if previous transaction is within GROUP_TIMEOUT (e.g., 300ms). If within window AND same 'group' (rapid typing), transact

per_question.expected_preview: The buffer validates that the anchor's buffer_id matches the snapshot's remote_id (lines 2306-2316 i
per_question.answer_preview: Before resolving an Anchor, Zed checks the anchor's buffer_id matches the BufferSnapshot being resolved against. If buffer_id mismatch, resolution panics or returns error (wrong buffer). Additional ch

per_question.expected_preview: The saved_version is updated in did_save (line 1529) and reload operations (line 1620-1621). When up
per_question.answer_preview: saved_version is updated only when buffer is successfully written to disk (save operation completes). At the same time, is_dirty flag is cleared (set to false). Constraint: saved_version = current ver

per_question.expected_preview: Editor
per_question.answer_preview: The main struct in the editor crate is Editor. It represents a text editor instance and handles rendering, input, selection management, and interaction with buffers.

per_question.expected_preview: gpui
per_question.answer_preview: The UI framework crate in Zed is gpui (General Purpose UI). It provides the application framework, rendering, event handling, and entity management.

per_question.expected_preview: Workspace
per_question.answer_preview: The main struct in the workspace crate is Workspace. It manages the overall application window, panes, docks, panels, and coordinates UI components.

per_question.expected_preview: MultiBuffer
per_question.answer_preview: The struct that represents multi-buffer in Zed is MultiBuffer. It enables displaying multiple buffers or excerpts as a single continuous text stream.

per_question.expected_preview: Project
per_question.answer_preview: The main struct in the project crate is Project. It manages the overall project state including buffers, worktrees, language servers, and coordinates between various stores.

per_question.expected_preview: Fs
per_question.answer_preview: The trait that represents a filesystem interface in Zed is Fs. It abstracts filesystem operations and has implementations for real filesystem and fake filesystem (for testing).

per_question.expected_preview: Buffer
per_question.answer_preview: The struct used to represent a text buffer is Buffer (in the language crate). It wraps the lower-level text::Buffer and adds language-specific features like syntax highlighting and LSP integration.

per_question.expected_preview: ThemeRegistry
per_question.answer_preview: The struct for managing themes is ThemeRegistry. It loads, caches, and provides access to themes throughout the application.

per_question.expected_preview: Rope
per_question.answer_preview: The main struct in the rope crate is Rope. It provides an efficient data structure for storing and manipulating large strings with O(log n) operations.

per_question.expected_preview: LanguageRegistry
per_question.answer_preview: The struct that represents the language registry is LanguageRegistry. It manages available languages, their configurations, and associated LSP adapters.

per_question.expected_preview: SettingsStore
per_question.answer_preview: The struct that handles settings is SettingsStore. It manages loading, caching, and providing access to all settings throughout the application.

per_question.expected_preview: BufferDiff
per_question.answer_preview: The main struct for buffer diff operations is BufferDiff. It computes and tracks differences between buffer content and a base version (like git diff).

per_question.expected_preview: main
per_question.answer_preview: The main function is located in crates/zed/src/main.rs. This is the entry point for the Zed application.

per_question.expected_preview: AppState
per_question.answer_preview: AppState in the collab crate holds shared application state for the collaboration server, including database connections, configuration, and service references needed by request handlers.

per_question.expected_preview: HighlightId
per_question.answer_preview: The struct that represents an individual highlight ID is HighlightId. It's a numeric identifier that maps to syntax highlighting styles defined in themes.

per_question.expected_preview: This module does not exist in Zed. While Zed uses sqlez (a custom SQLite wrapper) for structured dat
per_question.answer_preview: There is no SQLite database integration module in Zed's main storage layer. Zed uses the filesystem directly for buffer storage and settings files (JSON). For persistence like workspaces and sessions,

per_question.expected_preview: No such protocol exists in Zed. Zed uses a CRDT-based (Conflict-free Replicated Data Type) approach
per_question.answer_preview: Zed does not use a 'BlockchainCollab' protocol. There is no blockchain technology in Zed's codebase. Zed uses CRDT (Conflict-free Replicated Data Types) for collaborative editing, which ensures eventu

per_question.expected_preview: This crate does not exist in Zed. Zed has no GraphQL-related crates. While it has rpc and cloud_api_
per_question.answer_preview: There is no GraphQLQueryBuilder crate in Zed. Zed does not use GraphQL. For API communication, Zed uses Protocol Buffers (protobuf) for RPC messages between client and server. The collab server has RE

per_question.expected_preview: No such crate exists in Zed. While Zed integrates with multiple language model providers (anthropic,
per_question.answer_preview: There is no MachineLearningSuggestionEngine crate in Zed. Zed does not train ML models locally on user patterns. For AI features, Zed connects to external language model providers (OpenAI, Anthropic)

per_question.expected_preview: This crate does not exist in Zed. While Zed uses moka for in-memory caching in various places, there
per_question.answer_preview: There is no MemcachedCacheLayer crate in Zed. Zed does not use Memcached. File system operations use the local filesystem directly through the Fs trait. Caching in Zed is done through in-memory data s

per_question.expected_preview: No QuantumRenderEngine crate exists in Zed. Zed uses the gpui (graphical programming UI) framework w
per_question.answer_preview: There is no QuantumRenderEngine in Zed. GPU rendering in GPUI is handled through platform-specific backends: Metal on macOS, Vulkan on Linux, Direct3D on Windows. Shader implementations are in the gpu

per_question.expected_preview: No such crate or feature exists in Zed. Zed does not have PostgreSQL integration or connection pooli
per_question.answer_preview: Zed's client application does not have a PostgreSQLConnectionPool - it connects to the collab server via RPC, not directly to PostgreSQL. The collab server uses PostgreSQL with the sqlx crate for data

per_question.expected_preview: This crate does not exist in Zed. Zed does not have Kubernetes integration or distributed orchestrat
per_question.answer_preview: There is no KubernetesDeploymentManager crate in Zed. Zed does not use Kubernetes for plugin execution. Extensions run locally via WASM sandboxing in the extension_host crate. There's no distributed n

per_question.expected_preview: This module does not exist in Zed. While Zed has search and file_finder capabilities, there is no ve
per_question.answer_preview: There is no VectorDatabaseIndexer in Zed's codebase. Zed doesn't have built-in vector database or embedding-based semantic search. Search in Zed is text-based using ripgrep for file search and standar

per_question.expected_preview: This crate does not exist in Zed. Although Zed has a session crate for managing editor sessions loca
per_question.answer_preview: There is no RedisSessionManager crate in Zed. Zed does not use Redis. Session/workspace state is persisted locally using SQLite (db crate) and JSON files. Cross-instance synchronization happens throug
